ops.title: cco adopt P5 smoke: two defects found (tmux session collision + lsof race)
ops.body:
Live tmux adopt smoke (2026-07-18) passed all core adopt assertions but surfaced two real defects worth fixing before ship.

**1. tmux backend session-name collision across repos (functional, likely-common).**
`backend/tmux.go:37-38` `CreateWorkstream` names the tmux session `spec.Name` (the workstream/branch name) with no repo namespacing. Every auto-created repo's primary workstream is `main`, so a second repo whose primary workstream is `main` fails: `tmux new-session -d -s main ... exit status 1: duplicate session: main`. Hit during relocate adopt (Smoke B) while Smoke A's repo (its `main` session) was still active. Not adopt-specific — any two repos both on branch `main` collide on the tmux backend. Fix: namespace the session name per repo/workstream-id (the workstream ID `main-<hash>` is already unique) or scope by repo. Adopt itself is correct; it just creates a second `main` repo.

**2. adopt discovery aborts on a transient `lsof` exit-1 race (robustness).**
`procs.go:89` runs `lsof -a -p <all-claude-pids> -d cwd -Fn` and treats ANY non-zero exit as fatal (`list claude process working directories: exit status 1`). On a busy multi-agent machine (the fleet churns short-lived claude subagents), a claude PID from the `ps` snapshot can exit before the `lsof` call, and macOS `lsof -p <list>` exits 1 when it can't report on one listed PID — aborting `cco adopt` with an InternalError. Reproduced once as the first `cco adopt --cwd` list call; 5 immediate retries all succeeded, and a manual lsof over the live PID set exited 0 — confirming a race, not a deterministic failure. The CLI's `waitAdoptReady` does NOT retry list errors (only the `NotReady` conflict), so a single race aborts the whole adopt. Fix: tolerate `lsof` partial-failure (parse the output it produced) or query PIDs individually, and/or retry list errors in the poll loop.

Everything else worked end to end (in-place + relocate, kill/teardown, message round-trip, transcript continuity). See the smoke report for the full PASS matrix.</body>
<parameter name="paths">["orchestrate/procs.go", "backend/tmux.go", "orchestrate/adoptcmd.go"]
